ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-06-2023 Off Calendar

PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR ISSUANCE OF TENTATIVE RULING ONLY)
Off Calendar Jun-29-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than July 13, 2023, plaintiff must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims as to each defendant. No later than July 13, 2023, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on August 24, 2023 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than September 1, 2023. Responses to the demand are due no later than September 15, 2023. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Pang Ly via electronic mail by October 6, 2023 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, October 12, 2023 at 9 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed in open court. Judge: Samuel K. Feng, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/SKF)

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Jun-29-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Taylor Matsumoto of BERKES CRANE SANTANA & SPANGLER LLP (213-955-1150), appearing for defendant Foley-PMI, Inc.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). Conditional opposition filed by Intervenor Great American Insurance Company. Reply filed by Plaintiff. The trial date is October 16, 2023 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is October 27, 2023. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is October 12, 2023. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is September 29, 2023. Expert discovery cut-off date is October 13, 2023. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than July 27, 2023. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

A case management conference is set for July 7, 2022 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by August 12, 2022 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, August 18, 2022 at 9 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar May-05-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Hanna M. Thompson of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant Riley Power, Inc.
Jonathan Kim of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant D.W. Nicholson.
Jon C. James of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant Abbett Electric Corporation.

RULING:
Not contested, not argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under Code of Civil Procedure section 36(a). Conditional non-opposition filed by Defendant D.W. Nicholson Corporation.

The trial date is August 22, 2022 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is September 2, 2022. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court.

The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is August 18, 2022. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is August 5, 2022. Expert discovery cut-off date is August 19, 2022. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than June 3, 2022. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)
